content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. Let's define a function f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) in the following way:


If 1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N1 \le l \le r \le N1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N, then f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) is the maximum of Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶,ArA_l, A_{l+1}, \ldots, A_rAl‚Äč,Al+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ar‚Äč.
Otherwise, f(l,r)f(l, r)f(l,r) is 000.
Chef wants to choose a subset SSS of the set of indices {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N} such that ‚ą£S‚ą£‚Č•K|S| \ge K‚ą£S‚ą£‚Č•K. Let's define C=‚ąĎi‚ąąS‚ąĎj‚ąąSf(i,j)C = \sum_{i \in S}\sum_{j \in S} f(i, j)C=‚ąĎi‚ąąS‚Äč‚ąĎj‚ąąS‚Äčf(i,j).

Can you help Chef find the smallest value of CCC he can get?
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the minimum value of CCC.
